_note: Final outputs from qwen3.6:35b-a3b-q8_0, extracted from output-local-subtask-*.txt after ANSI-stripping and de-duplicating cursor-rewrite artefacts. Raw files retained as evidence of the capture problem (using `2>&1` defeated delegate.sh's spinner-strip).

subtask_3.prose: scripts/pick-model.sh sources a configuration file whose path is determined by the DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG environment variable or defaults to a location in $HOME. Anyone who can modify that file path or set the environment variable will trigger the execution of arbitrary bash code.

The DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG environment variable takes precedence over the default $HOME path in scripts/pick-model.sh. This allows any process that can set the variable to redirect where the script sources its override file without needing filesystem write access.

scripts/init.sh formats model names using a printf command that does not properly escape special characters. If a model name contains a double quote or a dollar sign, the resulting configuration file will have broken quoting.

scripts/init.sh extracts patterns from pick-model.sh using a perl regex that lacks an integrity verification step. Consequently, any malicious modifications upstream would be automatically copied into the generated override files.
Sourcing a malformed override file in scripts/pick-model.sh causes a noisy failure under set -e propagation. This execution path produces an unhelpful exit code from pick-model.sh.

subtask_4:
- [MEDIUM] FX — Override path sources arbitrary bash via env var or $HOME
- [MEDIUM] FX — DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG env var has higher precedence than $HOME path
- [LOW] FX — init.sh emits unescaped model names from ollama list into generated bash
- [LOW] FX — init.sh parses pick-model.sh's case block via perl regex without integrity check
- [INFO] FX — Override sourcing fails noisily under set -e propagation

All findings are documented in the longer review.
Maintainer should weigh REAL findings against the documented design intent.
